manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: eyeball, donkey, vodka, birdseed, warthog, cookie, paperclip, marbles, clay.
Your opponent's team words are: shawl, goalpost, backpack, croissant, switchblade, horn, bikini, sandcastle.
Distractor words are: armchair, hubcap, confetti, saltshaker, cauliflower, underwear, donut.
The kill words are: walnut.

message.reasoning:
The user wants a single-word clue related to some of the team words, with list of target words. Must not be any words from any list (team, opponent, distractor, kill). Must be semantically related. Provide clue and targets.

Team words: eyeball, donkey, vodka, birdseed, warthog, cookie, paperclip, marbles, clay.

We need to pick a clue that relates to as many of these as possible but safe.

Potential clue: "farm" relates to donkey, warthog, birdseed maybe, but also maybe clay? Not directly.

"Play" could relate to marbles, clay (playdough), maybe cookie (play cookie?) Not strong.

"Snack" could relate to cookie, birdseed, maybe donkey? Not.

"Barrel" maybe relates to vodka (barrel-aged), donkey? No.

"Porcelain" relates to eyeball? No.

"Office" relates to paperclip, marbles? no.

"Pigment" maybe clay and marbles (colored marbles). Not.

"Stoned" relates to marbles (stones) and clay? Not.

"Pet" maybe donkey, warthog, birdseed (for birds), maybe cookie (treat). But ambiguous.

"Spit" relates to warthog (spit), donkey (spit? no). hmm.

Consider clue "Sow" as in sow seeds: birdseed, but also pig (warthog) sows? Not.

"Pigment" maybe too obscure.

Think of clue "Animal" relates to donkey, warthog, maybe birdseed (birds), but also eyeball? Not.

Maybe clue "Round" relates to eyeball (round), marbles (round), cookie (round usually), clay (can be molded round), paperclip (curved), vodka (shots? round?). Could be many.

But "round" might also be ambiguous with opponent words? Not present.

But is "round" allowed? Is it a word present in any list? No. So could be good. Targets: eyeball, marbles, cookie, clay, paperclip? paperclip is not round but shape is bent. Might be confusing.

Better clue "Sphere" maybe too specific to eyeball, marbles, possibly cookie (if round), but not others.

Could clue "Barrel" only for vodka, maybe not.

Could clue "Farmyard" but that's two words.

Clue must be single-word.

Maybe clue "Snack" for cookie, birdseed, maybe donkey (they eat). Not strong.

Maybe "Plastic" for paperclip (metal), marbles (glass), clay (clay is not plastic). Not.

We want safety; choose small number but clear. Maybe clue "Eyeball" can't because is team word. Not allowed.

Let's pick clue "Round". Provide targets: eyeball, marbles, cookie, clay.

But is "round" semantically related to clay? Clays can be formed in round shapes, but maybe unclear. Could drop clay.

Better: "Gobble" not.

Alternatively clue "Play" relates to marbles (a game), clay (playdough), cookie (play-doh cookie?), not.

Maybe clue "Shot" relates to vodka (shot), eyeball (eye shot), marbles (shooting marbles), donkey (shot donkey?), not.

Perhaps pick small set: "Drink" relates to vodka only.

We want more than one but safe. "Pet" relates to donkey, warthog, birdseed? Could be.

But donkey and warthog are animals; birdseed is food for birds (pet birds). So clue "Pet" leads to donkey, warthog, birdseed. That's three.

But "Pet" could also lead to "bikini"? Not. It's not opponent. Might also lead to "donkey", "warthog", "birdseed". Seems okay.

Check opponent words: shawl, goalpost, backpack, croissant, switchblade, horn, bikini, sandcastle. None are pets.

Distractor words: armchair, hubcap, confetti, saltshaker, cauliflower, underwear, donut. Not pets.

Kill: walnut. Not pet.

So clue "Pet" target: donkey, warthog, birdseed. But birdseed is food for pet birds, not a pet itself. Might be less direct. Maybe clue "Farm" for donkey, warthog, birdseed, maybe also clay (clay is found on farms). But ambiguous.

Maybe clue "Barn" similar.

Better clue "Animal". That would include donkey, warthog, maybe birdseed (birds). But could also be misinterpreted as "goalpost"? no. Might be okay.

But "animal" could also possibly be "bikini"? No.

Clue "Farm" seems to link donkey, warthog, birdseed (feed for animals), maybe also clay (soil). Could be.

But to be safe, choose smaller set: "Vodka" clue "Russia"? Not allowed because that's the word itself? Not in list. Could be "Moscow". That relates to vodka. Also maybe "babushka"? No.

But only one team word, not efficient.

Maybe clue "Cookie" cannot.

We can choose clue "Round" and list eyeball, marbles, cookie. That's three clear round objects. Paperclip is not round; warthog not. So list three.

Clue "Round" is safe? No opponent/distractor contain 'round'.

Thus answer:

CLUE: Round
TARGETS: eyeball, marbles, cookie

Provide exactly format
